surface.name: Get started with Akamai Cloud, create and connect to a first Linode
surface.selection_basis: The current Akamai Cloud Get started guide explicitly says it covers account signup, Cloud Manager, first services, and billing. Its first-service section directs users to Create a Linode. The current Get started with Linodes page then orders Create a Linode, Connect to the Linode, and only afterward says the Linode can now be used for applications.

alternatives_considered.name: Quick Deploy App
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The creation guide presents Quick Deploy Apps as a peer source and says some take up to 30 minutes. It explicitly assumes a Distribution for its general path.

alternatives_considered.name: Linode API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Linodes overview lists the API as a peer deployment interface, while the linked general creation guide is specifically the Cloud Manager workflow.

alternatives_considered.name: Linode CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Linodes overview lists CLI as a peer interface, but the current create guide provides the complete browser path without first requiring a personal access token and CLI installation.

alternatives_considered.name: Akamai App Platform
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a Kubernetes application platform path requiring an LKE cluster and a multi-node minimum, not the general first compute-service journey.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an Akamai Cloud account, provision a first Linux virtual machine, and establish an interactive remote shell on it.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has a deliverable email address and a phone capable of receiving an SMS verification code.
The developer can provide an accepted credit or debit card or PayPal payment method and billing address, and can accept the Master Services Agreement.
For the recommended passwordless route, the local machine can run an SSH client and `ssh-keygen`, and the developer can securely retain a private key and passphrase.
The developer is authorized to incur hourly compute charges or use an applicable promotional credit.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Now you can start using your Linode
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Akamai Cloud has provisioned and fully booted the first Linode, and the developer has connected to its Linux shell through SSH or the built-in Lish console.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Linode detail page shows the instance finished provisioning and booted, and the developer reaches its root shell using the SSH Access command or reaches a login prompt through Lish.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Now you can start using your Linode

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Get started with Linodes places Connect to the Linode immediately after creation and begins Deploy new applications with the statement that the Linode can now be used. Application installation, OS hardening, DNS, and workload deployment are therefore after this first usable infrastructure success.

prerequisites.requirement: A working email address and SMS-capable phone number for account confirmation and fraud-prevention verification.
An accepted payment method and billing address. The signup guide lists credit or debit card and PayPal, and says the payment method is used to verify identity and secure the account.
Acceptance of the Master Services Agreement during signup.
An SSH client on the local machine for the primary passwordless connection path. Current Windows 10 and 11, macOS, and Linux all have documented terminal routes.
An SSH key pair and uploaded public key for the recommended passwordless route, or a strong root password for the documented alternative.

primary_path.action: Open Get started with Akamai Cloud and follow Sign up for an account.
primary_path.input: https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started
primary_path.output: The official ordered account and first-service path is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide shows Sign Up for an Account, Navigate Cloud Manager, Create your first Linode, and Understand billing.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Existing account holders can skip directly to Cloud Manager.

primary_path.action: Open the signup form and create credentials, or choose Google or GitHub signup.
primary_path.input: Email, unique username, and strong password; alternatively a Google or GitHub identity followed by the separate username prompt.
primary_path.output: A pending Akamai Cloud account registration exists.
primary_path.success_signal: The flow sends a confirmation email to the supplied address.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Username uniqueness, password requirements, provider authorization, or provider-account state can block progression.

primary_path.action: Open the confirmation email and click its verification link.
primary_path.input: The confirmation message sent shortly after registration.
primary_path.output: The email address is confirmed.
primary_path.success_signal: The signup flow advances to identity verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Email delivery delay or an inaccessible mailbox pauses the path.

primary_path.action: Enter a phone number, receive the SMS code, and enter that code in the signup form.
primary_path.input: SMS-capable phone number and one-time verification code.
primary_path.output: The phone-based identity check completes.
primary_path.success_signal: The signup form accepts the verification code and proceeds.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unsupported phone numbers, SMS delivery delay, or an incorrect or expired code can block activation.

primary_path.action: Choose Business or Personal account type and provide its required identity fields.
primary_path.input: Business requires company name and optionally Tax ID; Personal omits Tax ID.
primary_path.output: The account is classified for billing and administration.
primary_path.success_signal: The Billing section becomes available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing Business adds the company-name field, but the guide says platform features are the same for both account types.

primary_path.action: Select the payment method, enter billing-address details, and accept the Master Services Agreement.
primary_path.input: Accepted credit or debit card or PayPal details, billing address, and agreement acceptance.
primary_path.output: A payment method is recorded for identity verification and future usage charges.
primary_path.success_signal: The registration can be submitted for activation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An invalid payment method, address mismatch, or failure to accept the agreement prevents activation. No service charge occurs until a paid service is deployed.

primary_path.action: Activate the account or route it to manual review.
primary_path.input: The completed identity, phone, account-type, billing, and agreement information.
primary_path.output: An active account with access to Cloud Manager, or a pending manual review.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can sign in to Cloud Manager and add services.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Most accounts activate instantly, while a small number require manual review before any service can be created. The guide does not publish review duration or additional evidence requests.

primary_path.action: For the recommended passwordless route, generate an Ed25519 SSH key pair if one does not already exist.
primary_path.input: `ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "user@domain.tld"`, default or custom file path, and a secure passphrase.
primary_path.output: A local private key and matching `.pub` public key exist.
primary_path.success_signal: The public key file can be displayed and the private key remains on the local machine.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A developer may instead use an existing supported key or choose the root-password branch, but the official secure-setup guide recommends Ed25519 with a passphrase.

primary_path.action: Add the public SSH key to the Akamai Cloud user profile if it is not already stored.
primary_path.input: Profile, SSH Keys, Add an SSH Key, a descriptive label, and the public-key text, then Add Key.
primary_path.output: The public key is stored in Cloud Manager and is available for new Linodes.
primary_path.success_signal: The key appears on the SSH Keys page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Only the public key should be pasted. An unsupported format or malformed value is rejected.

primary_path.action: Open the Create Linode form.
primary_path.input: Cloud Manager, Create dropdown, Linode.
primary_path.output: The Create form is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: Region, image, plan, security, networking, and summary controls are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account must be active and permitted to create compute resources.

primary_path.action: Select a core compute region.
primary_path.input: A region near the developer or intended users, with the desired service and plan availability.
primary_path.output: The data-center location and its available plans are fixed for this Linode.
primary_path.success_signal: Plan options become selectable for the region.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Pricing and product availability can vary by region. Distributed compute regions use a separate guide.

primary_path.action: Choose a supported Linux Distribution image.
primary_path.input: A distribution such as a current Ubuntu LTS, Debian, or another supported image.
primary_path.output: The Linode will be provisioned as a bare Linux system from the selected distribution.
primary_path.success_signal: The image appears in the creation summary.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Quick Deploy Apps, StackScripts, custom images, backups, and clones are separate branches. The current general guide explicitly assumes Distribution.

primary_path.action: Choose a Linode type and plan.
primary_path.input: A plan sized for the intended evaluation and selected region; Shared CPU is the cost-efficient family and starts at $5 per month on the current product page.
primary_path.output: vCPU, memory, storage, transfer, hourly rate, and monthly cap are assigned.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected plan and price appear in the summary.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Plan availability and price can vary by region. The docs recommend starting with a smaller plan when uncertain because the VM can be resized later.

primary_path.action: Set the Linode label and optionally add tags; leave placement-group assignment for later if none exists.
primary_path.input: A valid label using letters, numbers, underscores, dashes, and periods; optional tags.
primary_path.output: The Linode has an account-visible identity.
primary_path.success_signal: The label passes validation and is shown in the summary.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Creating a placement group diverts to a separate interface, so the official guide recommends creating the Linode first for ease of use.

primary_path.action: Select the stored SSH public key for passwordless root access.
primary_path.input: One or more account SSH keys; omit Root Password on the recommended passwordless branch.
primary_path.output: The public key will be installed for the root user during provisioning.
primary_path.success_signal: At least one authentication method is present in the creation summary.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documented alternative is a strong Root Password. Weblish requires a root password even if the Linode was provisioned with keys only.

primary_path.action: Choose the required networking options for direct SSH access.
primary_path.input: Public Internet as the default interface, an auto-assigned public IPv4 address, and Linode Interfaces or Configuration Profile Interfaces as the required interface type.
primary_path.output: The Linode will have reachable public networking and an IPv4 address.
primary_path.success_signal: The public interface and interface type appear in the summary.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting only VPC or VLAN without public access prevents direct internet SSH. A VPC can add public IPv4 through 1:1 NAT.

primary_path.action: Select `No firewall - traffic is unprotected (not recommended)` for the minimal first-connection route.
primary_path.input: `No firewall - traffic is unprotected (not recommended)`.
primary_path.output: The Linode creation summary records that the public interface has no Cloud Firewall.
primary_path.success_signal: The no-firewall selection is recorded and creation can continue without a separate firewall-creation flow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This official option leaves public traffic unprotected and is explicitly not recommended. The recommended alternative is to create or select a Cloud Firewall before deployment; a firewall that drops SSH blocks the primary terminal.

primary_path.action: Review the Linode Summary and click Create Linode.
primary_path.input: Region, Distribution, plan, label, authentication, networking, firewall, and any optional settings.
primary_path.output: A paid compute service is created and the detail page opens automatically.
primary_path.success_signal: The detail page begins showing deployment status and assigned IP addresses.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An account still awaiting activation cannot create the service; the selected creation guide does not enumerate other create-time errors. Charges begin when the paid service is deployed. A promotional credit applies only when the account is eligible under the separate offer and promotion rules.

primary_path.action: Provision the selected Distribution image and fully boot the Linode.
primary_path.input: The accepted creation configuration.
primary_path.output: A running Linux VM with its assigned public IP and SSH key installed.
primary_path.success_signal: The creation and initialization finish and the Linode is ready for connection.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The creation guide says Distribution images can take about 3 minutes. If the Linode is not running or lacks a login prompt, use Cloud Manager, Lish, and the basic-connection troubleshooting guide.

primary_path.action: Copy the SSH Access command from the Linode detail page and run it locally.
primary_path.input: A command like `ssh root@192.0.2.1`, the selected private key, and the key passphrase if configured.
primary_path.output: An encrypted SSH session to the first Linode.
primary_path.success_signal: The remote Linux root shell prompt appears, establishing the current docs' Now you can start using your Linode boundary.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If SSH times out, is rejected, or rejects login, use Weblish or Lish to inspect power, boot, networking, sshd, firewall, root-login, password, and authorized-key state, then retry.

candidate_paths.name: Create a Distribution-based Linode in Cloud Manager and connect over SSH
candidate_paths.selection_basis: The platform-wide Get started guide chooses a first Linode, the current creation guide explicitly assumes Distribution, and both the Linodes overview and secure-setup guide make SSH the normal connection path after provisioning.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: This is the general current browser path and follows Akamai's recommended SSH-key authentication. It reaches a usable remote shell without installing an application, which the Linodes guide places after the boundary.

branches.path: Use email, username, and password, or continue with Google or GitHub and then supply a username.
branches.effect_on_first_success: All routes converge before email and phone verification.

branches.path: Choose Business and supply company name with optional Tax ID, or choose Personal without Tax ID.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The guide says feature access is identical; only signup fields differ.

branches.path: Most accounts activate instantly; a small number wait for manual review.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Manual review creates an unbounded gate before any resource can be deployed.

branches.path: Choose Quick Deploy App, StackScript, custom image, backup, or clone instead of a Distribution and follow its specific guide.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes configuration fields and can increase provisioning time from about 3 minutes to up to 30 minutes for some Quick Deploy Apps.

branches.path: Enter a strong root password instead of selecting an SSH key.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes local key generation and upload, permits Weblish, and uses password authentication, but conflicts with Akamai's passwordless recommendation.

branches.path: Use VPC with Allow public IPv4 access through 1:1 NAT rather than a direct Public Internet interface.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds VPC and subnet choices but can still converge on direct SSH.

branches.condition: The developer follows the recommended Cloud Firewall route
branches.path: Create a Cloud Firewall before Linode creation or from the linked Create Firewall form, configure rules that preserve the required SSH access, then select that firewall instead of the unprotected option.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a separate firewall configuration flow before deployment and reduces public exposure, but an overly restrictive rule can block the SSH terminal.

branches.condition: SSH is unavailable or the developer prefers a console
branches.path: Launch Weblish from the detail page if a root password exists, or use the Lish SSH gateway.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Reaches the same server shell through the serial console rather than the public SSH service.

friction_gates.description: Email confirmation is mandatory before identity verification can finish.
SMS phone verification is a mandatory fraud-prevention gate.
A payment method and billing address are required during signup even before a paid service is deployed.
A small number of accounts require an undocumented-duration manual activation review.
Recommended passwordless access adds local key generation, private-key retention, and a passphrase decision.
The developer must choose among multiple plan families and sizes whose availability and pricing vary by region.
A safe first public instance requires firewall judgment. Skipping the firewall is possible but explicitly not recommended; an overly restrictive firewall can block SSH.
A Distribution image takes about 3 minutes to provision before connection.
Lish is the documented out-of-band fallback for network or SSH failure and can be used to inspect boot, sshd, firewall, root-login, and authorized-key state.

time_to_first_success.value: The Create a Linode guide says Distribution images can take about 3 minutes to deploy. No total signup-to-shell estimate is published, and manual account review can add an unspecified delay.

sources.title: Create a Linode
sections_used: Open the Create form in Cloud Manager
Choose a distribution, app, or image
Choose a Linode type and plan
Set the label, add tags, and assign to a placement group

sources.title: Set up and secure a Linode
sections_used: Create and upload your SSH key

sources.title: Akamai Cloud Computing and CPU Pricing
sections_used: Find the right compute for your needs and workloads

sources.title: Akamai Technologies Completes Acquisition of Linode
evidence_supported: Akamai Technologies, Inc. acquisition and ownership of Linode
Relationship between current Akamai Cloud and Linode platform names

sources.title: Cloud Computing Services Free Offer
sections_used: Try Akamai Cloud with a US$100 Credit

uncertainties.question: What additional evidence, duration, and decision criteria apply when a new account is routed to manual review?
uncertainties.impact: A minority of users encounter an unbounded gate before the first service can be created.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current Get started guide acknowledges manual review but does not document its steps or timing.

uncertainties.question: Which Distribution and exact plan should be considered the universal tutorial default?
uncertainties.impact: The selected OS packages, hourly price, and instance capacity vary between valid first paths.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The general creation guide deliberately allows any supported Distribution and suitable regional plan; it provides examples but does not designate one canonical pair.

uncertainties.question: Does every signup entry point currently receive the advertised US$100 promotional credit, and for how long?
uncertainties.impact: The effective cost before first success and urgency of cleanup can differ.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The free-offer page makes the offer subject to separate promotion rules, while the universal account guide does not state that a credit is automatic.

excluded_after_success: Installing or deploying an application on the Linode.
System package updates, timezone and hostname configuration.
Creating a limited sudo user and hardening or disabling root SSH access.
Configuring a host firewall beyond what is needed for the first connection.
Adding DNS, domains, TLS, backups, monitoring, private networks, storage, or load balancing.
Enabling account two-factor authentication, though the signup guide recommends it.
Stopping billing by deleting the Linode after the evaluation.
